entities.label: Power 1910 EETS edition of Treatises of Fistula in Ano
notes: Power prints Sloane 6 as the principal English witness, supplies two defective stretches from Sloane 277, and adds extensive modern introduction and notes.
After Sloane 6 stops abruptly at the end of p. 102, Power prints a charm on pp. 103-104 from an unnamed different source.

rights.basis: The edition was first published in 1910. U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A states that works published in the United States before 1 January 1931 are in the public domain and describes a maximum 95-year term for pre-1978 statutory protection.
rights.component: The 1910 transcription, introduction, notes, apparatus, typography, and included manuscript plates

entities.description: A second-quarter fifteenth-century English paper medical miscellany. The ten-part Middle English Arderne compilation occupies ff. 141r-174v within a 205-folio codex.

entities.label: British Library Sloane MS 6

reviewNotes: The Latin source writings, anonymous Middle English translations, physical manuscripts, Power's 1910 edition, its 1968 lithographic reprint, U-M encoding, repository artifact, and reproductions are separate layers.
Sloane MS 6 is the principal witness. Sloane MS 277 is a direct source only for passages Power explicitly substitutes where Sloane 6 is defective; it is not modeled as a universal parallel base.
The complete Sloane 6 cache covers all 427 IIIF canvases at a documented derivative request size. The cataloged compilation maps to ff. 141r-174v, canvases 289-356, within that full codex.
Current U-M No Copyright-United States language and the restrictive 1999 availability notice embedded in the XML are both preserved rather than silently reconciled.

lineage.summary: The repository artifact is a local copy/version of U-M's 1999 ArderneFistula electronic text, keyed from the 1968 lithographic EETS reprint of D'Arcy Power's 1910 edition. Power prints the anonymous Middle English compilation in British Library Sloane MS 6, ff. 141r-174v, but supplies two substantial lacunae from the different English version in Sloane MS 277 and adds a charm from an unidentified source after Sloane 6 ends. A complete 427-canvas IIIF facsimile of the whole Sloane 6 codex and a complete PDF of the 1910 edition are publicly retrievable and cached locally with exact-source records.

catalogSummary.author: John Arderne as author of the Latin source treatises; anonymous Middle English translators
catalogSummary.date: Latin fistula treatise dated 1376; main English translation after 1413 and attested in Sloane MS 6, 1425-1449
catalogSummary.region: England; exact place and dialect of translation unresolved
catalogSummary.title: Treatises of Fistula in Ano, Haemorrhoids, and Clysters

catalogingSubject.label: Treatises of Fistula in Ano, Haemorrhoids, and Clysters
catalogingSubject.scope_note: The cataloged unit is the ten-part Middle English compilation of John Arderne's medical writings edited by D'Arcy Power: principally the anonymous translation in British Library Sloane MS 6, with two lacunae supplied in 1910 from the different English version in Sloane MS 277. It is distinct from Arderne's underlying Latin treatises, each physical witness, Power's introduction and notes, the separately sourced charm printed on pp. 103-104, the 1910 and 1968 printed editions as material publications, U-M's 1999 electronic encoding, and the repository XML artifact.

contentParts.label: Fistula in ano
notes: Power supplies two substantial gaps within this section from Sloane MS 277 on pp. 20-23 and 26-29.
contentParts.summary: Six topics on the surgeon, instruments, causes, definition, prognosis, operative techniques, after-treatment, and difficult anal fistulas.

contentParts.label: Fistula in the limbs
contentParts.summary: Seven topics on fistulas and related swellings or wounds in fingers, joints, legs, knees, feet, ankles, arms, and shin bones.

contentParts.label: Of Mormales
contentParts.summary: One topic defining and treating mormales, chronic ulcerative lesions of the legs.

contentParts.label: Of Piles
notes: The U-M encoding header warns that the table of contents and main text disagree around the transition from mormales to piles and that a supplied heading was inserted.
contentParts.summary: One topic on haemorrhoids and their treatment.

contentParts.label: Of Tenesmus
contentParts.summary: One topic on tenesmus and anal fissures and their treatment.

contentParts.label: Of Prolapse
contentParts.summary: One topic on rectal prolapse and its treatment.

contentParts.label: Of Clysters
contentParts.summary: One treatise on clysters, their apparatus, ingredients, administration, and uses.

contentParts.label: Of Powders
notes: Power describes this last remedy treatise as fragmentary in the present English translation compared with fuller Latin material in other manuscripts.
contentParts.summary: Nine topics on vitriol, alum, verdigris, arsenic and orpiment, and named medicinal powders or preparations.

contentParts.label: Of Oils and Waters
contentParts.summary: Thirteen topics giving recipes and properties for oils, ointments, waters, plasters, and plant preparations.

contentParts.label: Of Valences
notes: Sloane MS 6 ends abruptly after topic 44 on f. 174v; Power's charm on pp. 103-104 comes from an unidentified different source and is outside this core part.
contentParts.summary: Four topics on preparations of scabious and wormwood and on inducing and reversing sleep.

dateStatements.display: 1376 for Arderne's underlying Latin treatise on fistula in ano

scope.description: Underlying Latin source composition, not the date of the anonymous English translation

assertion.notes: The manuscript production range is not necessarily the translation date; it supplies only an upper bound for the extant copy.

dateStatements.display: After 20 March 1413; before or during production of Sloane MS 6 in 1425-1449

scope.description: Main Sloane MS 6 English version; Power derives the terminus post quem from an interpolation referring to the late Henry IV

dateStatements.display: Main English compilation attested in the second quarter of the fifteenth century (1425-1449), Sloane MS 6

scope.description: Principal Sloane MS 6 version, excluding Power's later editorial substitutions from Sloane MS 277

extent.description: Ten core medical sections and 44 numbered topics in the Sloane 6 table and Power edition. Counts exclude Power's front matter, separately sourced charm, appendix, notes, and index; they are intellectual/encoding structure metrics rather than a claim that every manuscript has identical divisions.

formStatements.description: The ten-part medical compilation is prose; verse quoted in Power's editorial notes is outside the cataloged medieval unit.

genres.label: Medical compilation
notes: Power states that Arderne's individual Latin treatises were later collected in varying combinations; Sloane 6 presents the ten-part grouping cataloged here.

languageStatements.label: Latin
notes: The Latin source works remain a distinct expression; this statement records only substantive embedded Latin in the English compilation.

scope.description: Medical terms, formulas, glosses, and short passages embedded in the Middle English prose

notes: The XML header lists Latin, Greek, French, and German, but those labels largely concern embedded terms, quotations, and Power's apparatus. Only Middle English as primary language and substantive embedded Latin are promoted to whole-record discovery languages.
Printed page ranges follow Power's stable pagination as retained in the 1968 lithographic reprint; they are not manuscript folio ranges.
The current BL component description's ff. 141r-174v range supersedes Power's narrower bibliography entry ff. 141-154v for the Practica alone when mapping the full ten-part compilation.

next_steps: Consult Peter Murray Jones's full 1989 study of the four Middle English translations and an item-level linguistic description in MEMT or the Linguistic Atlas tradition.
Keep translator identity and dialect region unassigned until directly supported.
openQuestions.question: Who produced the main Sloane 6 translation, when and where was it translated, and what dialect profile should be assigned to it independently of the witness's copying place?

next_steps: Check Power's surviving papers or later critical scholarship for an identification of the unnamed source.
Do not bind the charm to Sloane MS 6 or Sloane MS 277 without evidence.
openQuestions.question: Which manuscript did Power use for the Latin and English charm printed on pp. 103-104 after Sloane MS 6 ends?

next_steps: Compare Sloane MS 6 with Power's cited fuller witnesses, especially Ashmole MS 1434 and Digby MS 161, using a modern textual study.
openQuestions.question: How much of the final remedy material was absent from the translator's exemplar, omitted by the translator, or lost from Sloane MS 6?

assertion.notes: The broad cultural origin does not identify a dialect region or exact place of translation.

scope.description: Middle English translation tradition represented by an English-produced witness

notes: This source-author role does not attribute the anonymous English translation or Power's editorial wording to Arderne.

scope.description: Authorship of the underlying Latin treatises represented by this Middle English compilation

responsibilities.agentName: Anonymous Middle English translator represented by Sloane MS 6
responsibilities.display_name: Anonymous Middle English translator represented by Sloane MS 6

scope.description: The main Middle English compilation, except passages supplied by Power from Sloane MS 277

responsibilities.agentName: Anonymous Middle English translator represented by Sloane MS 277
responsibilities.display_name: Anonymous Middle English translator represented by Sloane MS 277

scope.description: Passages printed on pp. 20-23 and 26-29 where Power states that defective leaves of Sloane MS 6 are supplied from a different English version in Sloane MS 277
Power's 1910 edited composite and its 1968 lithographic reprint
Advice on the conduct, presentation, and fees of the surgeon

summaries.value: A Middle English prose compilation of John Arderne's practical medical writings, centered on diagnosis and surgery for fistula in ano and extending to fistulas elsewhere, mormales, haemorrhoids, tenesmus, prolapse, clysters, powders, oils, waters, and compound remedies. The work combines procedural instruction, case histories, recipes, surgical instruments, prognosis, and advice on professional conduct.

The Sloane 6 table and Power's edition organize the compilation into ten sections containing 44 topics. The remedy section is textually incomplete in this translation, and Power adds a Latin and English charm after the manuscript text ends abruptly.

The familiar author label 'John Arderne' identifies the Latin source author. The Middle English wording is anonymous, and Power's printed text is composite because pages 20-23 and 26-29 replace losses in Sloane 6 with another English version from Sloane 277.

notes: This is the established short title of Power's edition and the U-M item, not a claim that the medieval compilation circulated under this exact modern wording.
titles.value: Treatises of Fistula in Ano, Haemorrhoids, and Clysters

notes: Heading at Sloane MS 6, f. 141r, reproduced as the table-of-contents heading in Power's edition.
titles.value: A tretis extracte of Maistre Iohn Arden of fistula in ano and of fistula in oþer placeȝ of þe body and of apostemeȝ makyng fistuleȝ and of emoraideȝ & tenasmon and of clistereȝ: of certayn oyntementeȝ poudreȝ & oyles

Treatises of Fistula in Ano and of Fistulae in Other Parts of the Body, and of Apostemes Making Fistulae, and of Haemorrhoids and Tenasmon, and of Clysters, Also of Certain Ointments, Powders and Oils

work.summary: A Middle English prose compilation of John Arderne's practical medical writings, centered on diagnosis and surgery for fistula in ano and extending to fistulas elsewhere, mormales, haemorrhoids, tenesmus, prolapse, clysters, powders, oils, waters, and compound remedies. The work combines procedural instruction, case histories, recipes, surgical instruments, prognosis, and advice on professional conduct.
